stage.name: Open the local content ecosystem
stage.targetUser: Solo and small-team 2D game developers and technical artists who need to add legally traceable humanoid content without waiting for an application release
stage.qualityGate: A compatible third-party-style humanoid pack can be authored, validated, installed, versioned, used offline, transferred between hosts, removed safely, and credited exactly without renderer branches or silent project drift

promises.statement: A developer can inspect and install a compatible local humanoid content pack without an application release.
promises.user: Developer extending the available character catalog
promises.context: The developer has a local .spritepack file from themselves or another creator
promises.trigger: They choose Add pack from the pack library
promises.outcome: The product validates the complete package before mutation, shows identity, version, coverage, token bindings, provenance, and risks, then installs the exact content for offline use
promises.quality: Install is transactional, deterministic, host-consistent, and completes within 10 seconds for the maximum supported package on the reference machine
promises.recovery: Invalid, incompatible, duplicate, oversized, or interrupted installs preserve the source file and existing pack repository and return a stable error with a next action
promises.evidence: Shared adversarial fixtures plus actual-UI install, rejection, update, removal, and offline runs pass on web and packaged Electron

promises.statement: A technical artist can turn LPC-compatible humanoid layer sheets into a reusable, token-aware, legally attributed pack without writing JSON.
promises.user: Technical artist or developer with compatible original or redistributable layer art
promises.context: The source art follows the current 64px humanoid idle/walk sheet profile
promises.trigger: They create a pack draft and import one or more PNG layer sheets
promises.outcome: A guided workflow captures metadata, slots, fixed and token colors, coverage, source and license information, previews the exact runtime composition, and exports a deterministic .spritepack
promises.quality: Drafts autosave locally, validation points to exact assets and fields, and exported bytes are reproducible from an unchanged draft
promises.recovery: Decode, validation, quota, interruption, or export failure retains the draft and original imports and offers retry, remove offending asset, or export recovery data
promises.evidence: Actual-UI authoring, reload, validation, correction, preview, export, and self-install flows pass with exact render and credit fixtures

promises.statement: A developer can manage installed pack versions without silently changing or stranding existing projects.
promises.user: Returning developer with projects locked to installed content
promises.context: One or more local packs and projects exist
promises.trigger: They inspect, enable, disable, update, activate, or remove a pack
promises.outcome: Versions install side by side, projects preserve exact locks, destructive actions expose affected projects, and missing locks have explicit locate, install, replace-copy, or cancel recovery
promises.quality: No lifecycle command mutates project pack locks implicitly; current projects and last-known-good content remain available through failures
promises.recovery: In-use removal is blocked; unavailable content leaves the project intact in a deterministic blocked state
promises.evidence: Version, dependency, remove, missing-lock, rollback, and regression fixtures pass on both hosts

promises.statement: A developer can move authored packs and projects that use them between web and Electron without content, rendering, or provenance drift.
promises.context: A project references an installed local pack
promises.trigger: They export/import a .spritepack or transfer a .spriteproject containing embedded required content
promises.outcome: Both hosts resolve the same pack identity and bytes, produce identical render and export hashes, and generate the same merged credits
promises.quality: Canonical pack payload hashes, project locks, selected assets, runtime pixels, and credits match exactly across hosts
promises.recovery: A missing, conflicting, or tampered pack fails before project mutation and preserves both sources
promises.evidence: Bidirectional pack and project round trips pass from actual web and packaged Electron UIs

scenarios.startingState: Pack library contains bundled packs only
scenarios.intent: Inspect and install a compatible local pack
scenarios.environment: Supported web or packaged Electron host
scenarios.success: The exact pack is installed, offline-ready, and available to new recipes

scenarios.startingState: Existing projects and packs are valid
scenarios.intent: Understand why a pack cannot be installed
scenarios.environment: Malformed, hostile, unsupported, or oversized .spritepack fixture
scenarios.success: The package is rejected with a stable code and no repository or project mutation

scenarios.startingState: Bundled and locally installed pack versions exist
scenarios.intent: Inspect availability, origin, storage, usage, and status
scenarios.success: The library accurately distinguishes bundled, installed, embedded, disabled, missing, and in-use versions

scenarios.actor: Developer evaluating a newer pack version
scenarios.startingState: Projects lock version 1 and a valid version 2 package is available locally
scenarios.intent: Install the new version without changing existing projects
scenarios.success: Versions coexist and only explicit project action changes a lock after preview and checkpoint

scenarios.startingState: An installed pack may be unused or referenced
scenarios.intent: Remove only content that is safe to remove
scenarios.success: Unused versions remove after confirmation; referenced versions remain intact with affected projects named

scenarios.startingState: Project requires an unavailable exact pack lock
scenarios.intent: Recover deterministic rendering without losing the project
scenarios.success: Installing or locating the exact checksum resumes the project; replacement creates a copy and preserves the original lock

scenarios.actor: Technical artist with original compatible art
scenarios.startingState: No draft or a blank draft
scenarios.intent: Define pack identity and add humanoid layer sheets
scenarios.success: A draft contains valid metadata and at least one decoded, classified asset

scenarios.startingState: A valid asset sheet is present
scenarios.intent: Bind source colors to semantic tokens while preserving fixed colors
scenarios.success: Every source color has an explicit fixed or token+shade disposition and preview matches exported runtime pixels

scenarios.startingState: Assets are configured but legal metadata may be incomplete
scenarios.intent: Record exact source, author, offered license, chosen license, and attribution text
scenarios.success: Validation proves every asset has supported, internally consistent provenance and generated credits

scenarios.startingState: A dirty or previously failed draft exists
scenarios.success: The latest committed draft and imported bytes reopen, validation state recomputes, and recovery export is available

scenarios.startingState: One host authored a pack and project using it
scenarios.success: Pack bytes, project locks, preview hashes, generic/Godot exports, and credits remain identical

scenarios.actor: Developer receiving a project without a separately installed pack
scenarios.startingState: Project archive v2 embeds every non-bundled exact pack and destination is offline
scenarios.intent: Open and use the project without globally installing embedded content
scenarios.environment: Disconnected supported web or packaged Electron host
scenarios.success: Embedded exact locks validate project-scoped, preview/save/archive/generic/Godot/credits complete, and no global pack or enabled-state mutation occurs

capabilities.statement: User can choose a local .spritepack file without granting arbitrary path authority.
User can inspect pack identity, version, subject, assets, coverage, tokens, provenance, size, and warnings before install.
User can transactionally install a validated exact pack version for offline use.
User can see a stable actionable rejection without changing existing content.
User can list bundled, installed, embedded, disabled, missing, and in-use pack versions.
User can enable or disable an installed pack for new selections without changing existing locks.
User can install multiple versions of one pack side by side.
User can explicitly change a project to a compatible installed version after preview and checkpoint.
User can remove an unused installed version after explicit confirmation.
User can see why an in-use version cannot be removed and which projects depend on it.
User can locate or install the exact missing pack checksum and resume a blocked project.
User can replace an unavailable pack only in a new project copy after compatibility preview.
User can create a pack draft with stable ID, SemVer version, name, description, and humanoid subject profile.
User can import a compatible PNG layer sheet and retain the original bytes in the draft.
User can assign asset ID, display name, slot, description, and idle/walk coverage.
User can classify each source color as fixed or bind it to a semantic token and shade.
User can preview the authored asset through the same runtime renderer used by installed packs.
User can record supported license and attribution metadata for every asset.
User can rely on transactional local draft autosave and reopen imported source bytes.
User can download recovery data or remove an offending asset when a draft cannot validate.
User can validate the complete pack and navigate directly to every blocking field or asset.
User can export a deterministic .spritepack and matching human-readable validation report.
User can install the exported pack as a test copy before distributing it.
User can transfer authored packs and dependent projects between web and Electron with exact parity.

navigation.label: Packs
navigation.backBehavior: Returns to the prior workspace without changing project locks

navigation.label: Author pack
navigation.backBehavior: Preserves the draft and returns to its last completed section

navigation.label: Workspace
navigation.backBehavior: Returns to the active project without pack mutation

views.name: Pack library
views.purpose: Inspect and manage bundled, installed, embedded, disabled, missing, and in-use packs

views.name: Pack install review
views.purpose: Validate and summarize an untrusted pack before repository mutation

views.name: Pack version detail
views.purpose: Inspect content, origin, usage, versions, coverage, storage, and credits

views.name: Pack authoring workspace
views.purpose: Build a current-profile humanoid pack without editing JSON

views.name: Asset configuration
views.purpose: Configure one sheet, slot, coverage, color bindings, and preview

views.name: Pack validation and export
views.purpose: Resolve blockers and export or self-install a deterministic package

views.name: Missing pack recovery
views.purpose: Recover an exact project lock without silent substitution

views.name: Character workspace
views.purpose: Compose and export with bundled or installed packs through one renderer

expectedBehaviors.oracle: Add pack opens a native or browser file chooser limited to .spritepack; cancellation returns focus and mutates nothing.

Validation completes before install is enabled and shows exact ID, version, checksum, subject profile, asset count, complete coverage, token bindings, licenses, expanded size, warnings, and duplicate state.
Install commits manifest, pack document, provenance, and content-addressed PNG bytes in one host transaction, reads them back, and announces the installed checksum.
The installed pack appears in the pack library and new-recipe selector and renders through the shared runtime with no application reload or renderer branch.

Every named malformed, hostile, incompatible, or over-limit fixture fails at a documented validation stage with stable pack-invalid, pack-limit, unsupported-version, unsupported-license, or image-invalid code before mutation.
Existing packs, pack blobs, projects, drafts, and the selected source file remain byte-identical after rejection or interruption.
The error surface names the stage, offending entry or field, observed and allowed values, and offers choose another file, download validation report, or cancel.
Pack library lists every local version by name, ID, SemVer, checksum prefix, origin, enabled state, storage, availability, and dependent-project count with deterministic sorting and filters.
Pack detail shows assets by slot, runtime coverage, token mappings, source/license records, installed versions, and all exact project dependencies.
Disabling removes a pack only from new selections; existing locked projects continue rendering and exporting, and re-enabling restores selection availability.
A different valid SemVer installs beside prior versions; the same ID+version+checksum is idempotent and the same ID+version with a different checksum is rejected as pack-conflict.
Version detail identifies added, removed, and changed asset IDs and names every project that would become incompatible before any project action is enabled.

Use version creates a named pre-change snapshot, validates every selected asset, previews semantic/render/credit differences, and changes only the active project's exact lock after explicit confirmation.
Cancel writes nothing; restoring the pre-change snapshot restores the prior exact lock, recipe, pixels, and credits.
An unused non-bundled version removes only after a confirmation naming identity, checksum, and bytes; shared blobs remain while referenced by any other version.
An in-use, bundled, embedded-only, or currently authoring test version cannot be removed; the UI names every dependency and offers open project, export backup, or cancel.
Failed removal retains the complete version and blobs, reports a stable error, and leaves dependent projects renderable.
Opening a project with an unavailable lock preserves and displays the project but blocks deterministic preview/export, names exact ID/version/checksum, and never substitutes another version.
Locate exact pack accepts only a package whose identity and payload checksum match the lock, installs it transactionally, and resumes the unchanged project.
Replace in a copy requires an installed compatible version, previews missing assets and output changes, creates a new project ID with checkpoint, and leaves the original project and lock unchanged.
Cancel returns to the blocked project with all source data intact and no pack or project mutation.
Create pack produces a local draft with generated stable draft ID, user-defined canonical pack ID, valid SemVer, humanoid-lpc-64 profile, revision zero, and visible Unsaved/Saving/Saved state.
Add layer sheet accepts PNG only, verifies signature and exact 256x512 RGBA dimensions before decode, computes SHA-256, stores original bytes, and rejects duplicates without partial assets.
Asset configuration requires unique canonical ID, name, description, one current humanoid slot, and complete idle/walk four-direction profile; errors remain adjacent and retain input.
The color table lists every distinct non-transparent RGBA color with pixel count and requires fixed or token+shade mapping; no hidden automatic mapping is committed.
Every asset requires author, source title, HTTPS source URL, offered supported licenses, one chosen offered license, and attribution text when the chosen license requires it.
Preview uses the shared installed-pack renderer and displays idle/walk in all directions, token theme changes, pixel hash, coverage, and generated credit record from the draft bytes.

Meaningful metadata, asset, mapping, and provenance edits mark Unsaved within one frame and commit draft metadata plus referenced source blobs after a 750 ms quiet period with integer revision conflict checks.
Browser restart or Electron relaunch reopens the latest committed draft, original imported bytes, active asset, and recomputed validation report without changing draft bytes.

Quota, write, decode, stale-revision, or read-back failure retains dirty state and last committed draft and offers retry, save copy, remove offending asset, download recovery, or cancel as applicable.

Validate checks package limits, identity, graph references, PNGs, slots, full coverage, every color disposition, provenance/licenses, runtime preview, deterministic credits, and canonical serialization and groups results by exact asset/field.
Activating a validation item opens the owning section, focuses the exact field or asset, preserves all draft inputs, and removes the item only after successful revalidation.
Export is enabled only with zero blockers and emits one deterministic .spritepack plus text validation report; unchanged drafts produce identical payload entry hashes on both hosts.
Install test copy validates the exported bytes through the public install path, marks origin authored-local, and makes the pack available without granting the draft special runtime behavior.
A web-authored .spritepack installs in packaged Electron with identical canonical payload hashes, asset inventory, preview hashes, and credits.
A project using the local pack transfers in .spriteproject with exact lock and embedded required content, opens offline in the other host, and produces identical generic/Godot outputs.
Electron re-exported pack and project reopen in web without conversion, host metadata leakage, payload drift, or duplicate installation.
Pack detail reports Ready offline only when manifest, provenance, and every referenced PNG checksum resolve locally.

After network disconnect and restart, installed pack browsing, selection, token recolor, preview, save, archive, generic export, Godot export, and credits complete with zero required network requests.
A deliberately incomplete local install reports the exact missing checksum and blocks use/export without fetching or substituting content.

flows.completion: Exact pack version is installed, offline-ready, and selectable

Trigger and recover from failed save or validation

Open fixture with missing local blob

exclusions.expectation: Generate terrain or add a second producer
exclusions.reason: The delivery roadmap opens the pack ecosystem in Phase 4 before using it for the Phase 5 terrain producer
exclusions.consequence: UPD-002 proves extensibility only with current-profile humanoid content
exclusions.disposition: Phase 5 terrain update after pack ecosystem evidence

exclusions.expectation: Compose characters and world assets in scenes
exclusions.reason: Scene value and schema depend on at least two real producers
exclusions.consequence: Projects continue to expose one active character recipe
exclusions.disposition: Phase 5 after terrain producer is specified

exclusions.expectation: Author creatures, mounts, animals, or rider composites
exclusions.reason: Distinct frame geometry, slots, masks, and z-order would make the pack profile ambiguous
exclusions.consequence: Subject profile is fixed to current LPC humanoid 64px sheets
exclusions.disposition: Later content ecosystem update from real non-humanoid fixtures

exclusions.expectation: Draw or edit source pixels in the product
exclusions.reason: Pack authoring configures compatible existing art; pixel/animation editing is a separate Phase 6 promise
exclusions.consequence: Users edit source PNGs in an external art tool and reimport

exclusions.expectation: Define custom frame sizes, layouts, directions, or animations
exclusions.reason: The next increment must prove the ecosystem without redesigning renderer geometry
exclusions.consequence: Assets use exact 256x512 current humanoid idle/walk layout
exclusions.disposition: Versioned future pack profile after a real second geometry

exclusions.expectation: Watch arbitrary source or pack folders for live changes
exclusions.reason: Watcher lifecycle and path trust are independent desktop-only concerns
exclusions.consequence: Authors explicitly reimport changed PNGs and re-export packs

exclusions.expectation: Browse, download, publish, rate, moderate, or sell packs through a hosted catalog
exclusions.reason: Marketplace requires backend, identity, moderation, legal, and operating commitments

exclusions.expectation: Install a pack directly from a URL
exclusions.reason: Remote retrieval adds network trust, availability, and privacy boundaries not needed for local ecosystem proof
exclusions.consequence: Users download a pack first and install the local file
exclusions.disposition: Future distribution update after local evidence

exclusions.expectation: Run pack scripts, plugins, shaders, SVG, or custom decoders
exclusions.reason: Executable content would violate the data-only local trust boundary
exclusions.consequence: Pack entries are strict JSON, PNG, and UTF-8 text only

exclusions.expectation: Add Unity, GameMaker, RPG Maker, or another engine adapter
exclusions.reason: An engine adapter has an independent user promise, implementation, evidence fixture, release, and rollback boundary
exclusions.consequence: Generic and Godot exports remain the supported adapters
exclusions.disposition: Candidate UPD-003 after adapter demand and target are selected

exclusions.expectation: Enter arbitrary custom license terms or receive legal clearance
exclusions.reason: Unbounded terms cannot be validated or converted into deterministic attribution rules
exclusions.consequence: Authoring supports an explicit curated license vocabulary and states that the product does not provide legal advice
exclusions.disposition: Expand only through reviewed license profiles

exclusions.expectation: Automatically convert existing third-party repositories into packs
exclusions.reason: Source layouts and licenses require deliberate human mapping and review
exclusions.consequence: Authors import and configure assets individually or from a bounded draft
exclusions.disposition: Future CLI after authoring contract is verified

workPackages.gate: Complete update envelope, product/UX contract, engineering contract, threat model, quality scenarios, exclusions, and ready spec review
Shared strict pack/draft schemas, canonical serializer, image profile, license profiles, limits, stable errors, and adversarial fixtures pass pure tests
Web and Electron repository adapters pass transactional install, side-by-side version, dependency, remove, and recovery contract suites
PNG-backed authored assets render through one host-neutral runtime with deterministic token replacement, frame, spritesheet, export, and credit hashes
Draft repository, asset import/configuration, token mapping, provenance, validation, preview, recovery, deterministic export, and self-install pass actual-UI flows
All pack lifecycle and authoring flows pass production web keyboard, reflow, reduced-motion, offline, quota, and failure evidence
Packaged Electron uses opaque file grants and validated userData pack storage with no renderer path or Node authority and passes equivalent lifecycle/authoring flows
Bidirectional pack/project transfer preserves canonical payload, lock, preview, generic/Godot, and credit hashes from actual hosts
All affected UPD-001 project, archive, offline, export, Electron security, and cross-host flows regress cleanly with zero severity 0-2 issues

environment.web: Playwright Chromium against a test-owned Vite production preview; 1440x900, 640x450 200%-equivalent reflow, exact 320x800 viewport, and service-worker-controlled offline restart
environment.desktop: Packaged Electron 40.10.6 Windows x64 portable ZIP on Windows 10.0.26220 with isolated userData, context isolation, sandbox, and frozen 18-method preload bridge
environment.quality: Vitest 4 strict schemas, canonical archives, hostile ZIP metadata, PNG decode/profile, deterministic package, fake-IndexedDB transaction, draft recovery, and bridge-boundary suites
environment.regression: All MVP and UPD-001 production browser/cross-host flows and packaged filesystem/close/security flows rerun locally
environment.performance: 66.29 MB near-boundary package in production Chromium and packaged Electron, plus 100 draft saves and 100 preview recolors
environment.pagesSimulation: Exact /the-sprite-project/ app and service-worker scope with docs at /the-sprite-project/docs/, online install, controlled reload, offline restart, and export

unverifiedReleaseGates: Actual GitHub Pages publication, smoke/offline run, and deployed rollback/withdrawal rehearsal
Independent clean-machine Windows launch, unsigned SmartScreen observation, and executable rollback

decisions.decision: UPD-002 opens the local humanoid content ecosystem before terrain, scenes, non-humanoids, or engine-adapter expansion.
decisions.evidence: docs/planning/overview.html Phase 4 and direct product-owner approval on 2026-07-20

decisions.decision: Use one deterministic data-only .spritepack ZIP format with independent pack container, pack schema, and draft schema versions.
decisions.evidence: UPD-001 canonical archive patterns and direct product-owner approval on 2026-07-20

decisions.decision: Format version 1 authoring accepts exact 256x512 RGBA PNG sheets for the current 64px humanoid idle/walk four-direction layout and current six slots only.
decisions.evidence: Current renderer geometry and direct product-owner approval on 2026-07-20

decisions.decision: Packs contain strict JSON, PNG, and UTF-8 text only; scripts, plugins, SVG, shaders, links, and custom decoders are rejected.
decisions.evidence: Local untrusted-input boundary and direct product-owner approval on 2026-07-20

decisions.decision: Pack ID, SemVer, and payload checksum form exact identity; versions install side by side and project locks change only through explicit checkpointed action.
decisions.evidence: UPD-001 exact-lock contracts and direct product-owner approval on 2026-07-20

decisions.decision: Authoring supports a reviewed finite license vocabulary with offered and chosen licenses plus deterministic attribution; arbitrary custom terms are excluded and the product does not provide legal advice.
decisions.evidence: LPC provenance research and direct product-owner approval on 2026-07-20

decisions.decision: The next engine adapter remains a separate update because target selection, output contract, downstream fixture, and rollback are independent of pack ecosystem delivery.
decisions.evidence: Anti-bundling governance and direct product-owner approval on 2026-07-20
